content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
February 24, 1987 
Mr. Dave Low 
Governmental Relations Representative 
California School Employees Association 
1127 11th Street, Suite 346 
Sacramento, 
CA 
95814 
Dear Mr. Low: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-87-032 
You have requested advice on behalf of the California School 
Employees Association concerning the reporting of campaign 
expenditures pursuant to the Political Reform Act.li 
QUESTIONS 
1. 
Must the Association report the payments for reproducing 
and mailing the Association's newsletter to members and officers 
if the newsletter includes articles requesting membership 
participation in partisan political activities (e.g., requesting 
members of the Association to volunteer to work on campaigns or to 
vote for or against a particular candidate)? 
2. 
Is an employer required to report reimbursements to 
employees for travel and meal costs incurred as a result of 
providing services for political purposes if these employees spend 
less than 10 percent of their time involved in this type of 
activity? 
CONCLUSIONS 
1. 
The Association will not incur a reporting obligation if 
the newsletter is sent only to its members and officers, is not 
issued on a date other than its regular schedule, and is not 
expanded in circulation or substantially altered in style, size or 
format. 
liGovernment Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to,the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative Code 
Section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code. 
~ 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


Mr. Dave Low 
Page 2 
2. 
Reimbursement for meals, travel, accommodations and other 
personal expenses to employees who spend more than 10% of their 
compensated services for political purposes is reportable. 
However, such payments on behalf of employees who spend 10% or 
less of their compensated time providing services for political 
purposes are not reportable. 
ANALYSIS 
(1) section 82025 states that an "expenditure" is: 
" ... a payment ... unless it is clear from the surrounding 
circumstances that it is not made for political 
purposes .. . " 
Regulation 18225(b) (copy enclosed) provides: 
(4) ... the term expenditure does not include costs 
incurred for communications which expressly advocate the 
nomination, election or defeat of a clearly identified 
candidate or candidates or the qualification, passage or 
defeat of a clearly identified measure or measures by: 
(C) 
A regularly published newsletter or regularly 
published periodical ... whose circulation is limited to an 
organization's members, employees, shareholders, other 
affiliated individuals and those who request or purchase 
the pUblication. 
This paragraph applies only to the 
costs regularly incurred in publishing and distributing 
the newsletter or periodical. 
If additional costs are 
incurred because the newsletter or periodical is issued 
on other than its regular schedule, expanded in 
circulation, or substantially altered in style, size or 
format, the additional costs are expenditures. 
(2) Regulation 18423 (copy enclosed) provides that: 
(a) 
The payment of salary, reimbursement for personal 
expenses, or other compensation by an employer to an 
employee who spends more than 10% of his compensated time 
in anyone month rendering services for political 
purposes is a contribution or expenditure .... 
Regulation l8423(d) further provides: 
This regulation does not affect the obligations of an 
employer or any other person to report expenditures and 
contribut~ons other than the salary, reimbursement for 
personal expenses, or compensation for personal services 
of an employee. 


Mr. Dave Low 
Page 3 
Although the compensation and reimbursement for personal 
expenses of an employee who does not spend more than 10% of his or 
her compensated time rendering services for political purposes are 
not reportable, any other costs incurred by the employer are 
reportable contributions or expenditures. 
For example, any 
photocopying costs provided to a candidate by the Association are 
reportable expenditures. 
If you have any further questions regarding this matter, 
please contact me at (916) 322-5662. 
By: 
DG:MAK:kmt 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
Diane Griffiths 
General Counsel 
Kvasager 
1 Reform Consultant 


CALIFORNIA SCHOOL EMPLOYEES ASSOCIATION 
Governmental Relations Office • 1127 11th St., Ste., 346 • Sacramento, CA.9,5~M\ :'§le16) 444-0598 
Jlb 'Ll 
~ if ,' .. I 
' 
January 22, 1987 
Ms. Diane Griffiths 
Chief Counsel 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
Dear Ms. Griffiths: 
\.H",;''% L.,J 
We are writing in request of an opinion whether certain 
expenditures are reportable under FPPC regulations. 
First, with regard to newsletters, we send certain newsletters to 
our union members and officers each month. 
One of our 
newsletters contains updates on legislation and another instructs 
leaders on activities they are requested to perform, such as 
visiting legislators or meeting with chapters. 
We would like to know whether it is a reportable expenditure if 
we include requests for partisan political activity in these 
newsletters. 
For example, if we request members to volunteer to 
work on campaigns or to vote for or against a candidate, must we 
report the cost of the newsletter? 
The request mayor may not 
generate volunteers or votes, and the members receive no 
compensation from the union. 
Second, with regard to staff volunteers, it is our understanding 
that a staff person can work for a candidate or campaign for less 
than 10% of their time and not have to report. 
What are the 
reporting requirements if this staff person must travel by car or 
plane to meet with campaign workers and is reimbursed by the 
union for travel costs and/or meals? 
As we may be involved in a special election in the next month, we 
would appreciate a reply as soon as possible. 
Thank you for your 
assistance. 
Sincerely, 
Dave Low 
Governmental Relations Representative 
THE NA TlON'S LARGEST CLASSIFIED SCHOOL EMPLOYEE ASSOCIA TlON 
@!> 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
January 26, 1987 
Dave Low 
Governmental Relations Representative 
California School Employees 
Association 
1127 - 11th Street, Suite 346 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Re: 
87-032 
Dear Mr. Low: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on January 23, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days. 
You also should be aware that your 
letter and our response are public records which may be 
disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for 
disclosure. 
JP:plh 
v~rYtrUIY ~r;s, 
. 
~-..::.:.rC~/V)v..- <:::I;J' vUv'L /.1\ 
~Jeanne Pritchard 
'jChief 
Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916) 322,5660
